steps.title: 28 Days
steps.body:
Twenty-eight days ago, you started a journey.

You didn't know where it would lead. You knew your marriage could be better — and you had the courage to begin.

Look at what you've done:

— WEEK 1: Diagnosis —
Day 1: You started with honesty
Day 2: You learned why couples drift and how bids work
Day 3: You audited your foundation — trust, respect, values
Day 4: You checked your emotional and physical connection
Day 5: You faced the household reality — money, labor, mental load
Day 6: You identified your conflict style and repair patterns
Day 7: You created your marriage blueprint and a vision

— WEEK 2: Repair —
Day 8: You committed to moving from diagnosis to action
Day 9: You learned the art of the do-over
Day 10: You faced the conversations you'd been avoiding
Day 11: You practiced listening without defending
Day 12: You mastered the apology that actually works
Day 13: You set boundaries that protect not push away
Day 14: You consolidated your repair toolkit

— WEEK 3: Connection —
Day 15: You put friendship back at the center
Day 16: You went deeper into vulnerability
Day 17: You brought play and lightness back
Day 18: You reclaimed touch as a language of connection
Day 19: You built rituals that anchor your relationship
Day 20: You understood how trust is built in small moments
Day 21: You celebrated what you'd rebuilt

— WEEK 4: Mission —
Day 22: You started defining your marriage mission
Day 23: You created your shared vision
Day 24: You designed the practices that will carry you
Day 25: You committed to growing together through change
Day 26: You became a team that wins together
Day 27: You practiced celebration and savoring

And now, Day 28. The day you declare who you are — together.

You didn't just read about marriage improvement.
You showed up, every day, for 28 days.

That's not a program.
That's a choice.

And you made it.

questions.label:
Go back to Day 1. Read what you wrote. Do you remember where you were?

Now answer:

• What's one belief you had about your marriage that has shifted?
• What's one conversation you had that you wouldn't have had 28 days ago?
• What's one habit you've changed?
• What are you most proud of?

This isn't about perfection. It's about progress. Name your progress.

steps.title: The Marriage Manifesto
steps.body:
A manifesto is a public declaration of intent, values, and vision.

Your Marriage Manifesto is a statement — for the two of you — that captures:

• Who you are as a couple
• What you value most
• What you're committed to
• Where you're going together

This isn't a contract. It's not a set of rules. It's a compass — something you can come back to when life gets noisy.

On the next step, you'll write yours.

questions.label:
Complete these sentences:

We are a couple who _____________________________

We value ______________________________________

We commit to ___________________________________

We are building ________________________________

Take your time. This is the most important writing you'll do in this program.

If you want a simpler framework:

'We promise to [action] even when [obstacle], because [reason].'

Example: 'We promise to turn toward each other even when we feel distant, because we chose this and we keep choosing it.'

Write your manifesto.

steps.title: Share Your Manifesto
steps.prompt:
Tonight, share your Marriage Manifesto with your partner.

Here's how to start:

'I wrote something that matters to me about us. I want to share it with you, and I'd love to hear yours too.'

Don't negotiate it. Don't debate it. Just share — then listen to theirs.

If you can, write a combined version. Keep it somewhere you can see it. On your mirror, in your wallet, on your phone.

When things get hard — and they will — your manifesto is your anchor.
steps.body:
Tonight, share your Marriage Manifesto with your partner.

Here's how to start:

steps.title: What Happens After Today
steps.body:
The program ends. Your marriage doesn't.

Here's what to do next:

1. Keep your daily practice — even 5 minutes. A check-in, a touch, a question. Don't let connection backslide.

2. Schedule your weekly huddle — 15 minutes. Win, challenge, plan. This alone will transform your marriage.

3. Revisit — Come back to the four pillars every few months. Foundation, Connection, Household, Repair. You'll find different things to work on in different seasons.

4. Recommit — When you slip, don't spiral. Your practices are there to come back to. A missed day isn't failure. It's a reminder.

This program gave you the tools. The rest of your life is where you use them.

You have everything you need.

questions.label:
Complete this sentence:

'The thing I want to remember most from these 28 days is...'

Write it. Memorize it. It's your takeaway — the one insight that matters most to you.

Everything else is practice. This one is yours.

steps.title: The Journey Continues
steps.body:
You did it. All 28 days.

Here's the truth: 28 days doesn't fix a marriage. But 28 days of showing up, learning, and practicing can change the direction of one.

You've built skills that will serve you for the rest of your life. Not because this program is perfect — but because YOU showed up.

The diagnosis is done. The repairs are in motion. The connection is deeper. The mission is clear.

Now go live it.

Together.

steps.title: Achievement Unlocked: DUO Graduate
steps.body:
28 days. Four weeks. A full journey from diagnosis to mission. You didn't just complete a program — you invested in the most important relationship of your life.

Badge earned: DUO Graduate

You've earned this. And more importantly — your marriage has you.

steps.title: Day Complete
steps.body: Great work today! You're building something real.
